items.q: What did Renaissance artists rediscover that medieval art lacked?
items.correct: Linear perspective and naturalistic representation of the human body.
items.why: Students assume medieval art was colourless. It was vivid but flat - the Renaissance breakthrough was mathematical PERSPECTIVE (Brunelleschi) and anatomical realism, not the introduction of colour.

items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.correct: Removing material from a solid block to reveal the form (e.g., carving stone).
items.why: Students confuse 'subtractive' with arithmetic. In sculpture, additive = building up (clay, welding); subtractive = taking away (carving). Michelangelo described 'freeing' figures from the marble - quintessential subtractive thinking.

items.q: Andy Warhol is most associated with:
items.why: Correct answer: Pop Art. “Cubism” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Sensation 1997. “Salon des Refusés” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Roughly in which decade did the YBAs become prominent?
items.why: The real figure is 1990. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
